methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's partnership with AI Ready RVA signals distributed capability-building through community-oriented education infrastructure, potentially creating peer learning networks beyond traditional tech hubs if the model proves replicable across Virginia regions.

signals.title: EPA grant backs community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia - Virginia Tech News
signals.signal_reading: EPA-funded community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia represents bottom-up capacity development in a historically underserved region, testing whether deep skill-building can take root outside metro concentration zones through local partnership models.

signals.title: AI is here in Hampton Roads, and businesses and organizations are embracing it - The Virginian-Pilot
signals.signal_reading: Broad business adoption of AI tools in Hampton Roads suggests platform-delivered shallow integration rather than deep capability development, indicating distributed but surface-level deployment that maintains rather than disrupts existing regional capability hierarchies.

signals.title: Northern Virginia Technology Council Urges Removal of Data Center Tax Repeal from State Budget - PR Newswire
signals.signal_reading: NoVA Tech Council's lobbying to preserve data center tax incentives reinforces infrastructure and capital concentration in Data Center Alley, deepening the metro advantage while policy choices actively channel AI-adjacent economic development away from distribution.

signals.title: Norfolk Southern deploys AI system to catch wheel defects before they break - The Business Journals
signals.signal_reading: Norfolk Southern's deployment of specialized AI defect detection systems represents deep technical capability concentrated in established industrial corridors, showing how AI adoption follows existing centers of technical sophistication rather than democratizing capability.
